text:
To determine the number of bulbs that are on after all rounds, we need to consider the factors of each bulb's position. A bulb will be toggled (switched on or off) on each round that corresponds to one of its factors. For example, bulb 12 will be toggled on rounds 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, and 12 because those are the factors of 12.

A bulb will end up in the "on" state if it is toggled an odd number of times. This happens when the bulb's position has an odd number of factors, which is true for perfect squares. Therefore, the number of bulbs that are on after all rounds is equal to the number of perfect squares between 1 and 100.

Verification checks:
1. Consider a smaller set of bulbs, such as 10 or 16, and manually toggle the bulbs according to the rules to verify the pattern.
